parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'A rotameter bobbin rises in a tapered tube until the upward forces generated by gas flow and pressure difference equal the downward force of its weight.'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"query": 'A rotameter bobbin rises in a tapered tube until the upward forces generated by gas flow and pressure difference equal the downward force of its weight.'}

{"topic":"Primary Frca Sample Sbas","exam":"primary","curriculum_node_code":null,"model":"openai:gpt-5.5","generated_at":"2026-05-02T01:16:45.156172","questions":[{"stem":"An oxygen rotameter bobbin reads 1 L min-1. As the needle valve is opened further the bobbin rises and then comes to rest at the 8 L min-1 marker.","lead":"Which description best explains why the bobbin comes to rest at the 8 L min-1 marker?","options":[{"text":"The cross-sectional area of the rotameter tube has increased","is_correct":false,"explanation":"The tapered tube's cross-sectional area increases as the bobbin rises, but this alone does not explain why it stops at a particular height."},{"text":"The flow has become less laminar","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Flow characteristics may change around the bobbin, but this is not the fundamental reason it comes to rest."},{"text":"The flow has become more turbulent","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Turbulence may increase at higher flows, but the bobbin stops because forces balance."},{"text":"The forces acting above and below the bobbin have equalised","is_correct":true,"explanation":"At equilibrium, upward force from the pressure difference and gas flow equals the bobbin's weight, so it remains stationary at that calibrated flow."},{"text":"The pressure gradient across the bobbin has remained constant during its ascent","is_correct":false,"explanation":"The pressure gradient does not simply remain constant throughout ascent; the bobbin moves until the opposing forces balance."}],"explanation":"A rotameter bobbin rises in a tapered tube until the upward forces generated by gas flow and pressure difference equal the downward force of its weight. A stationary bobbin indicates force equilibrium at the calibrated flow.","curriculum_node_codes":[],"curriculum_node_scores":[]}]}
